audit_items.proof_trace: All major allocation sites in nfp_net_common.c have symmetric frees on every code path: the kmalloc in nfp_net_clone_dp is freed at both exit_free_dp and err_cleanup_vecs in nfp_net_ring_reconfig; kcalloc/dma_alloc_coherent/kvcalloc in nfp_net_rx/tx_rings_prepare are correctly unwound via the goto-inside-while error patterns; and nfp_net_close_free_all covers the normal close path. The one stylistic concern—nfp_net_tx_ring_bufs_free using return (not continue) on NULL frag—is safe because XDP txbufs are filled sequentially during alloc and the page-swap in nfp_net_tx_xdp_buf never sets any txbuf frag to NULL during runtime.
